title: 3 Warren Buffett Dividend Growth Stocks to Buy Now and Hold Forever
text: Many of Warren Buffett's top holdings end up being dividend growth stocks. Such stocks have historically outperformed the overall market.

title: T-Mobile Just Eliminated the Biggest Factor Holding Its Stock Price Down. It Could Skyrocket in 2024.
text: T-Mobile's merger with Sprint left it with a big overhang on its stock price for years. It agreed to pay Softbank 48.8 million shares if it traded above $150 for 45 days.

title: T-Mobile Is the Best Telecom Stock for 2024
text: The company looks poised to continue gaining market share. Investors needn't worry about its plan to issue more shares.

title: T-Mobile (TMUS) Outperforms Broader Market: What You Need to Know
text: The latest trading day saw T-Mobile (TMUS) settling at $158.95, representing a +0.64% change from its previous close.

title: 3 Stocks Set for Success After Surviving Data Breaches in 2023
text: It's been a record year for companies hit by data breaches.  According to a November article from Nasdaq.com contributor Chris Morris, U.S. companies had reported 2,116 data breaches as of the end of September, 14% higher than the previous record of 1,862 in 2021.

title: SoftBank to receive $7.6B in T-Mobile stock at no cost
text: SoftBank Group Corp announced late Tuesday that it will receive shares in telco T-Mobile US, valued at about $7.59 billion, at no additional cost.  The Japanese tech conglomerate said it told T-Mobile US to issue 48.75 million of its common shares to SoftBank after conditions set out in an agreement made as part of the merger of SoftBank's US telco Sprint and T-Mobile were met.

title: SoftBank Up As it Receives 48.8 Million T-Mobile Shares
text: American depository receipts (ADRs) of Softbank were up about 2.5% Wednesday after the company said it would receive more shares of T-Mobile U.S. (TMUS) as part of the sale of Sprint.

title: T-Mobile is suddenly the industry gem with analyst target boosts
text: There are always stocks that feel very cold to the touch, especially shivering to those who cannot afford to buy thousands of shares to make every uptick count. Companies in the technology sector are typically not this type, as they usually carry high betas (move more aggressively).

title: Arm Holdings owner Softbank ends year on high with T-Mobile US boost
text: SoftBank, the owner of UK chip maker Arm Holdings, got a boost as it received shares worth US$7.6bn in T-Mobile US. The Japanese tech investor got the 48.75 million shares due to conditions set out when its US telco Sprint merged with T-Mobile being met, it said.

title: SoftBank shares jump 6% after exercising T-Mobile option
text: TOKYO, Dec 27 (Reuters) - SoftBank Group Corp (9984.T) said it would receive shares in telco T-Mobile US (TMUS.O) worth some $7.59 billion at no additional cost, driving the Japanese conglomerate's shares up 5%.

title: Buffett's 4 Dividend Buys For December
text: The article discusses the dividend-paying stocks in Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway portfolio. It highlights three stocks - The Kraft Heinz Co, Ally Financial Inc, and HP Inc - that have annual dividends exceeding their single share prices. The article also provides projected net gains for ten top Buffett-held dividend stocks by December 2024.

title: 3 Stocks to Buy as the Nasdaq 100 Hits New All-Time High
text: On Dec. 19, the NASDAQ 100 hit 16,772.71, an all-time high, with plenty of Nasdaq 100 stocks to buy. Up 53% year-to-date due to its surge in 2023, it is the tech-heavy index's best annual return since 2009.

title: T-Mobile: The New Telecom Leader
text: T-Mobile has grown into a significant telecom giant that distinguishes itself from the competition through a differentiated strategy and superior offering. TMUS's superior 5G network and aggressive pricing strategies have propelled its subscriber numbers and revenue, with a CAGR of 15% from 2012 to 2022. The US telecom market is projected to grow at a CAGR of 6.5%, and T-Mobile is well-positioned to benefit from this growth.

title: AT&T vs. T-Mobile: Which Is a Better Dividend Stock?
text: Both AT&T and T-Mobile offer reasons to invest, as each experienced strong customer and revenue growth in the third quarter. AT&T boasts a high dividend yield, but is mired in debt.

title: T-Mobile (TMUS) Stock Declines While Market Improves: Some Information for Investors
text: T-Mobile (TMUS) closed at $156.87 in the latest trading session, marking a -1.68% move from the prior day.

title: 3 tax-loss selling buys to start 2024 with double-digit gains
text: The end of the year brings with it portfolio adjustments, window dressing and tax harvesting. This results in fund managers and investors selling their losers and buying the winners.

title: Pre-Christmas Stock Picks: 3 Growth Stocks Analysts Are Gushing Over
text: Retail sales in the United Statesrecently exceeded forecasts, bolstered by strong manufacturing and robust consumer demand amid this ongoingeconomic recovery. The Federal Reserve Bank of Atlanta projects the strongest GDP growth since late 2021, indicating economic resilience.

title: T-Mobile Empowers 250 Communities Nationwide with $11 Million in Hometown Grants
text: BELLEVUE, Wash.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Un-carrier is coming through big time for 25 small towns. T-Mobile (NASDAQ: TMUS) today announced its latest round of Hometown Grant recipients, hitting a whopping 250 communities across 43 states and more than $11 million in funding since the Un-carrier kicked off its five-year commitment to small towns in April 2021. Through Hometown Grants, people in communities nationwide are kickstarting projects that make a real difference and help their towns thrive.

title: Telecom Titans: 3 Stocks Outperforming in the 5G Race
text: The nationwide rollout of the 5G mobile network three years ago marked a critical moment in telecommunications, ushering in a new era of investment in 5G stocks. This upgrade from 4G LTE to 5G represented a quantum leap in technology, promising download speeds up to 100 times faster.

title: T-Mobile (TMUS) Accentuates Multi-Band Spectrum Strategy
text: T-Mobile (TMUS) is testing 5G mmWave on 5G standalone network for crowded areas like stadiums and fixed wireless service to accentuate its multi-band spectrum strategy.
